constructors.name: Callable
constructors.summary: Constructs an empty [Callable], with no object nor method bound.

constructors.name: Callable
constructors.summary: Constructs a [Callable] as a copy of the given [Callable].

constructors.name: Callable
constructors.summary: Creates a new [Callable] for the method named [param method] in the specified [param object]. [b]Note:[/b] For methods of built-in [Variant] types, use [method create] instead.

members.name: Bind
members.summary: Returns a copy of this [Callable] with one or more arguments bound. When called, the bound arguments are passed [i]after[/i] the arguments supplied by [method call]. See also [method unbind]. [b]Note:[/b] When this method is chained with other similar methods, the order in which the argument list is modified is read from right to left.

members.name: Bindv
members.summary: Returns a copy of this [Callable] with one or more arguments bound, reading them from an array. When called, the bound arguments are passed [i]after[/i] the arguments supplied by [method call]. See also [method unbind]. [b]Note:[/b] When this method is chained with other similar methods, the order in which the argument list is modified is read from right to left.

members.name: Call
members.summary: Calls the method represented by this [Callable]. Arguments can be passed and should match the method's signature.

members.name: CallDeferred
members.summary: Calls the method represented by this [Callable] in deferred mode, i.e. at the end of the current frame. Arguments can be passed and should match the method's signature. [codeblocks] [gdscript] func _ready(): grab_focus.call_deferred() [/gdscript] [csharp] public override void _Ready() { Callable.From(GrabFocus).CallDeferred(); } [/csharp] [/codeblocks] [b]Note:[/b] Deferred calls are processed at idle time. Idle time happens mainly at the end of process and physics frames. In it, deferred calls will be run until there are none left, which means you can defer calls from other deferred calls and they'll still be run in the current idle time cycle. This means you should not call a method deferred from itself (or from a method called by it), as this causes infinite recursion the same way as if you had called the method directly. See also [method Object.call_deferred].

members.name: Callv
members.summary: Calls the method represented by this [Callable]. Unlike [method call], this method expects all arguments to be contained inside the [param arguments] [Array].

members.name: Create
members.signature: public Callable Create(Variant variant, StringName method)
members.summary: Creates a new [Callable] for the method named [param method] in the specified [param variant]. To represent a method of a built-in [Variant] type, a custom callable is used (see [method is_custom]). If [param variant] is [Object], then a standard callable will be created instead. [b]Note:[/b] This method is always necessary for the [Dictionary] type, as property syntax is used to access its entries. You may also use this method when [param variant]'s type is not known in advance (for polymorphism).

members.name: GetArgumentCount
members.summary: Returns the total number of arguments this [Callable] should take, including optional arguments. This means that any arguments bound with [method bind] are [i]subtracted[/i] from the result, and any arguments unbound with [method unbind] are [i]added[/i] to the result.

members.name: GetBoundArguments
members.summary: Returns the array of arguments bound via successive [method bind] or [method unbind] calls. These arguments will be added [i]after[/i] the arguments passed to the call, from which [method get_unbound_arguments_count] arguments on the right have been previously excluded. [codeblock] func get_effective_arguments(callable, call_args): assert(call_args.size() - callable.get_unbound_arguments_count() >= 0) var result = call_args.slice(0, call_args.size() - callable.get_unbound_arguments_count()) result.append_array(callable.get_bound_arguments()) return result [/codeblock]

members.name: GetBoundArgumentsCount
members.summary: Returns the total amount of arguments bound via successive [method bind] or [method unbind] calls. This is the same as the size of the array returned by [method get_bound_arguments]. See [method get_bound_arguments] for details. [b]Note:[/b] The [method get_bound_arguments_count] and [method get_unbound_arguments_count] methods can both return positive values.

members.name: GetMethod
members.summary: Returns the name of the method represented by this [Callable]. If the callable is a GDScript lambda function, returns the function's name or [code]"<anonymous lambda>"[/code].

members.name: GetObject
members.summary: Returns the object on which this [Callable] is called.

members.name: GetObjectId
members.summary: Returns the ID of this [Callable]'s object (see [method Object.get_instance_id]).

members.name: GetUnboundArgumentsCount
members.summary: Returns the total amount of arguments unbound via successive [method bind] or [method unbind] calls. See [method get_bound_arguments] for details. [b]Note:[/b] The [method get_bound_arguments_count] and [method get_unbound_arguments_count] methods can both return positive values.

members.name: Hash
members.summary: Returns the 32-bit hash value of this [Callable]'s object. [b]Note:[/b] [Callable]s with equal content will always produce identical hash values. However, the reverse is not true. Returning identical hash values does [i]not[/i] imply the callables are equal, because different callables can have identical hash values due to hash collisions. The engine uses a 32-bit hash algorithm for [method hash].

members.name: IsCustom
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this [Callable] is a custom callable. Custom callables are used: - for binding/unbinding arguments (see [method bind] and [method unbind]); - for representing methods of built-in [Variant] types (see [method create]); - for representing global, lambda, and RPC functions in GDScript; - for other purposes in the core, GDExtension, and C#.

members.name: IsNull
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this [Callable] has no target to call the method on. Equivalent to [code]callable == Callable()[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] This is [i]not[/i] the same as [code]not is_valid()[/code] and using [code]not is_null()[/code] will [i]not[/i] guarantee that this callable can be called. Use [method is_valid] instead.

members.name: IsStandard
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this [Callable] is a standard callable. This method is the opposite of [method is_custom]. Returns [code]false[/code] if this callable is a lambda function.

members.name: IsValid
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the callable's object exists and has a valid method name assigned, or is a custom callable.

members.name: Rpc
members.summary: Perform an RPC (Remote Procedure Call) on all connected peers. This is used for multiplayer and is normally not available, unless the function being called has been marked as [i]RPC[/i] (using [annotation @GDScript.@rpc] or [method Node.rpc_config]). Calling this method on unsupported functions will result in an error. See [method Node.rpc].

members.name: RpcId
members.summary: Perform an RPC (Remote Procedure Call) on a specific peer ID (see multiplayer documentation for reference). This is used for multiplayer and is normally not available unless the function being called has been marked as [i]RPC[/i] (using [annotation @GDScript.@rpc] or [method Node.rpc_config]). Calling this method on unsupported functions will result in an error. See [method Node.rpc_id].

members.name: Unbind
members.summary: Returns a copy of this [Callable] with a number of arguments unbound. In other words, when the new callable is called the last few arguments supplied by the user are ignored, according to [param argcount]. The remaining arguments are passed to the callable. This allows to use the original callable in a context that attempts to pass more arguments than this callable can handle, e.g. a signal with a fixed number of arguments. See also [method bind]. [b]Note:[/b] When this method is chained with other similar methods, the order in which the argument list is modified is read from right to left. [codeblock] func _ready(): foo.unbind(1).call(1, 2) # Calls foo(1). foo.bind(3, 4).unbind(1).call(1, 2) # Calls foo(1, 3, 4), note that it does not change the arguments from bind. [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator !=
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if both [Callable]s invoke different targets.

operators.name: operator ==
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if both [Callable]s invoke the same custom target.
